Executive opportunity layer
Size of the prize — headline indicators
Click any card to trace the opportunity
Spend rationalisation
Medium confidence
£4.2M
Addressable through supplier consolidation, entity normalisation, and category renegotiation across top-12 relationships.
Layers 1 → 2 → 3
Explore →
Payment efficiency
High confidence
£263K
Directly evidenced: credit note reduction (£263K recovered 2024→2025), with further early-payment discount opportunity if terms data is enriched.
Layers 1 → 2 → 3
Explore →
Commercial risk exposure
Indicative
£7.1M
Spend at risk from unconfirmed governance: Karl Steiger concentration (£3.6M), YEG entity ambiguity (£2.2M), Baltic labour model change (£1.3M).

Carbon / Scope 3 exposure
Directional
12,781t
Estimated Scope 3 CO₂e (2025). Equipment Hire highest-intensity sector at 0.55t/£1K — doubled in spend. HVO100 signal already present.
Layers 1 → 2 → 3
Explore →
Operational diagnosis Six insight lenses — click any to view detail
Supplier concentration
Karl Steiger GmbH at 7.6% of spend
Single relationship grew 214% YoY. PSI Global consolidates to £3.88M. True concentration understated in reporting.
Governance questionDetail →
Phase-driven spend shift
Electrical −45%, Mechanical +86%
Phase transition, not instability. Equipment Hire doubled (+105%). Supply base configured for current phase — not the next.
Sector viewDetail →
AP efficiency & disputes
Credit value fell 90% year on year
£293K credits in 2024 → £30K in 2025. 25.4% batch at 08:00. Improving AP behaviour; payment date data would unlock DPO analysis.
AP analysisDetail →
Carbon / Scope 3 leverage
Equipment Hire highest carbon intensity
0.55 tCO₂e per £1K — sector doubled in spend. ODN Oil BV HVO100 supply is a positive signal already in the dataset.
Carbon / Scope 3Detail →
Data enrichment value
~£11M understated concentration
Four relationships across 14 entity variants. Project / job code per transaction is the highest-value enrichment — shifts to project-centric view.
Minor BytesDetail →
Governance & commercial risk
SAS Transition OU & Intel Pay UAB exited
Two Baltic payroll intermediaries (£1.33M in 2024) absent in 2025. Labour model change warrants governance confirmation.
DI OutputDetail →
Primary signal — spend concentration, No-PO exposure, and AP processing patterns

Ask SPIE about this dataset
What's changed year on year? Where is supplier risk increasing? Which sectors are growing? What are the carbon signals?
Year-on-year · 2024–25 full year
What has changed and why
Like-for-like period. December 2024 (£3.34M) excluded. All movements interpreted through the Decision Integrity Framework.
Interpreting the contraction
Spend fell 10.9% and lines fell 15.3% — but average transaction value rose 5.2%. The portfolio is running fewer, higher-value transactions. This is the signature of milestone-based subcontract billing replacing high-frequency material and labour procurement. It is a phase signal, not a volume decline caused by demand reduction.
Spend movement by sector
£ change 2024 → 2025
Transaction value distribution
Line value bands
Key movements — six signals
Ask SPIE about this dataset
What's changed year on year? Where is supplier risk increasing? Which sectors are growing? What are the carbon signals?
Minor Bytes · Decision Integrity Framework
Enrichment that increases confidence
Minor Bytes are data characteristics that do not block analysis but would improve precision or depth if addressed. They are not errors. They are opportunities.
Framework position
The Decision Integrity Framework operates on the principle that most insight comes from a relatively small amount of decision-grade data. The analysis above is based on 80% spend coverage, like-for-like period comparison, and GBP base currency. None of the Minor Bytes above prevent the directional conclusions from being drawn. Each would sharpen a specific dimension if addressed — but none is a prerequisite.
Ask SPIE about this dataset
What's changed year on year? Where is supplier risk increasing? Which sectors are growing? What are the carbon signals?
Decision Integrity Output · generated from tool
Structured output view
The Decision Integrity Output is an exportable structured view generated from this tool's current state. It is not the tool — it is what the tool produces.
Generate Decision Integrity Output
Anonymised dataset · full year · current analysis state
The output will include: assumptions box, five structured sections, sector table with first-pass classification, supplier signals, Minor Bytes with priority ratings, and closing reflective prompts. It will open in a new tab for review, printing, or sharing.
What SPIE does not attempt to do This tool surfaces signals and patterns. It does not certify, audit, cleanse master data, or substitute for commercial judgement. Every signal it identifies is a starting point for a conversation — not a conclusion.

AP Analysis · Anonymised dataset · transactional behaviour signals
Processing patterns & AP intelligence
Invoice volume, credit signals, timing patterns, and operational consistency. Framed through the Decision Integrity Framework — imperfect fields treated as Minor Bytes, not blockers.
AP Minor Bytes — fields not available in this dataset
P1 — Payment dates: DPO and on-time payment rate cannot be calculated. Load payment data to unlock.
P1 — Receipt date: Processing time is a proxy (creation timestamp) only.
P2 — Approver/cost centre: Workload distribution by individual cannot be assessed.
P2 — PO reference: Invoice-to-PO matching rate unavailable. Zero-VAT used as indirect signal.

Lines · 2024 vs 2025

Transaction timing — hour of day
2025 · amber = peak 08:00 window
Activity by day of week
2025 · Mon–Fri pattern
Transaction value bands

Carbon · Scope 3 intelligence lens
Spend-based carbon signals
Directional signals only. Not a verified carbon audit. Not ESG scoring. Not compliance reporting.
Method: Spend-based EEIO proxy using DEFRA-derived intensity factors (tCO₂e per £1,000 spend) by sector. Scope 3 Category 1 — Purchased Goods & Services. Results are directional signals for internal decision-support only. Must not be used for public reporting or certification claims.
Estimated Scope 3 by sector
2025 · tCO₂e estimated
Spend % vs carbon % — divergence
Where carbon weight exceeds spend weight
Sector carbon profile
EI = emission intensity tCO₂e / £1,000 spend
Key carbon signal — Equipment Hire & Site Services
Equipment Hire doubled in spend (+105%) and carries a 0.55 tCO₂e / £1,000 intensity factor — the highest in the portfolio. Estimated Scope 3 from this sector rose from ~1,105t to ~2,263t. ODN Oil BV (new in 2025, £761K) supplies HVO100 sustainable fuel — a supply-side decarbonisation signal within this sector. Directional signal only.

Decision-making output · Layer 3 · Anonymised facilities dataset
From insight to action
Actions grouped by executive savings indicator. Every row rolls up to a headline opportunity figure. Self-contained for board packs and CFO meetings.

Compare spend, activity, and carbon intensity across selected sites. Use the site selector to isolate or compare specific locations. Sorted by total spend descending.
